entry.title: Hwiki: Home
entry.content:
Welcome to the public wiki section of my site.

Start Here#

  • Paper notes
  • Travel notes

About This Wiki#

This wiki is where I collect paper notes, short summaries, and research updates connected to my academic work.

Example Sections#

  • Travel notes
  • Example note: Washington, DC

entry.title: Washington, DC
entry.content:
This is a sample public note for a folder-based wiki section.

Why This Note Exists#

I use this page as an example of how to create a new public note inside a new folder in notes/.

Example Structure#

  • Folder: notes/travel/
  • Section entry page: Travel Notes
  • This note: Washington, DC

Example Links#

  • Back to Travel Notes
  • Back to Hwiki Home

entry.title: Travel Notes
entry.content:
This section collects short public notes on travel, cities, and places.

Notes#

  • Washington, DC

entry.title: News Press Landscape and Asymmetric Polarization
entry.content:
Citation#

Lee, Byunghwee, Hyo-sun Ryu, Jae Kook Lee, Hawoong Jeong, and Beom Jun Kim. 2025. Network analysis reveals news press landscape and asymmetric user polarization. Physica A.

Overview#

This paper studies the structure of the news press landscape and user polarization using network analysis. It reflects my broader interest in media systems, public discourse, and online collective behavior.

Why I Think It Matters#

  • It connects network structure to political and media dynamics
  • It offers a data-driven view of asymmetry in polarization
  • It contributes to my ongoing work on online discourse and social systems

Related#

  • Research updates and recognition

entry.title: Dissecting Landscape Art History with Information Theory
entry.content:
Citation#

Lee, Byunghwee, Min Kyung Seo, Daniel Kim, In-seob Shin, Maximilian Schich, Hawoong Jeong, and Seung Kee Han. 2020. Dissecting landscape art history with information theory. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences 117(43): 26580-26590.

Overview#

This paper uses information-theoretic methods to study long-term changes in the composition of landscape paintings. It is one of the central examples of my work at the intersection of data science, art history, and cultural analytics.

Why I Think It Matters#

  • It shows how quantitative methods can reveal broad patterns in visual culture
  • It bridges physics-inspired methods with art historical questions
  • It helped establish a line of work on computational art history in my research

Related#

  • Research updates and recognition

entry.title: Belief Embeddings with Large Language Models
entry.content:
Citation#

Lee, Byunghwee, Rachith Aiyappa, Yong-Yeol Ahn, Haewoon Kwak, and Jisun An. 2025. A semantic embedding space based on large language models for modelling human beliefs. Nature Human Behaviour.

Overview#

This paper develops a semantic embedding space based on large language models to model human beliefs and positions in public debate. The project connects representation learning with computational social science and provides a framework for studying relative belief structure at scale.

Why I Think It Matters#

  • It creates a computational representation of beliefs rather than reducing opinion to simple labels
  • It links language model representations to social and political judgment
  • It opens a path toward measuring belief change and persuasion in a structured way

Related#

  • Preprints and work in progress
  • Research updates and recognition

entry.title: Paper List
entry.content:
This page provides a small collection of paper notes drawn from my recent research.

Papers#

  • Belief embeddings with large language models
  • Dissecting landscape art history with information theory
  • News press landscape and asymmetric polarization
  • Preprints and work in progress

entry.title: Preprints and Work in Progress
entry.content:
Preprints#

  • LLMs Can Infer Political Alignment from Online Conversations (2026)
  • A Benchmark for Zero-Shot Belief Inference in Large Language Models (2025)
  • Context-aware Multimodal AI Reveals Hidden Pathways in Five Centuries of Art Evolution (2025)
  • Combined influence of artwork and artist features in predicting user generated art valuation (2025)

In Preparation#

  • The geometry of persuasion: Quantifying belief change in a latent embedding space

Notes#

These projects continue my work on belief representation, multimodal AI, art and culture, and political discourse.
